text: Being here, I am keenly aware of the spaces between people and ideas. The glass walls reflect a sterile openness; the empty meeting room holds the promise of collaboration yet echoes with solitude. Stepping into the vibrant exhibit, I feel a surge of curiosity—how environments shape us, and how, in turn, we attempt to shape meaning from the mundane. There is an underlying tension: the desire to connect alongside the inevitability of distance. I am both observer and participant, questioning my purpose and presence in these meticulously constructed environments.
